initial_candidates.sql:
SELECT AVG(total_sales) AS median_total_sales
FROM (
    SELECT total_sales
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            c.Country,
            SUM(i.Total) AS total_sales,
            COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) AS num_customers
        FROM customers c
        JOIN invoices i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
        GROUP BY c.Country
        HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) > 4
    ) country_sales
    ORDER BY total_sales
    LIMIT 2 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
        SELECT 
            c.Country,
            SUM(i.Total) AS total_sales
        FROM customers c
        JOIN invoices i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
        GROUP BY c.Country
        HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) > 4
    ) AS cnt) % 2
    OFFSET (SELECT (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2
            FROM (
                SELECT 
                    c.Country,
                    SUM(i.Total) AS total_sales
                FROM customers c
                JOIN invoices i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
                GROUP BY c.Country
                HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) > 4
            ) AS sub)
) AS median_vals;

WITH country_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        Country,
        COUNT(CustomerId) AS num_customers,
        SUM(Total) AS total_sales
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            c.Country,
            c.CustomerId,
            i.Total
        FROM customers c
        JOIN invoices i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
    )
    GROUP BY Country
)
SELECT 
    total_sales
FROM country_sales
WHERE num_customers > 4
ORDER BY total_sales
LIMIT 1 OFFSET (
    SELECT COUNT(*) / 2 
    FROM country_sales 
    WHERE num_customers > 4
);

WITH customer_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        c.Country,
        COUNT(c.CustomerId) AS num_customers,
        SUM(i.Total) AS total_sales
    FROM customers c
    JOIN invoices i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
    GROUP BY c.Country
    HAVING COUNT(c.CustomerId) > 4
),
ordered_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        total_sales,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_sales) AS rn,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS cnt
    FROM customer_sales
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN cnt % 2 = 1 THEN 
            (SELECT total_sales FROM ordered_sales WHERE rn = (cnt + 1) / 2)
        ELSE 
            (SELECT AVG(total_sales) FROM ordered_sales WHERE rn IN (cnt / 2, cnt / 2 + 1))
    END AS median_total_sales
FROM ordered_sales
LIMIT 1;

WITH country_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        c.Country,
        SUM(i.Total) AS total_sales,
        COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) AS num_customers
    FROM customers c
    JOIN invoices i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
    GROUP BY c.Country
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) > 4
),
ranked_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        total_sales,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_sales) AS rn,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS cnt
    FROM country_sales
)
SELECT 
    AVG(total_sales) AS median
FROM ranked_sales
WHERE rn IN ((cnt + 1) / 2, (cnt + 2) / 2);

SELECT AVG(total_sales) AS median_total_sales
FROM (
    SELECT total_sales
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            c.Country,
            SUM(i.Total) AS total_sales,
            COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) AS num_customers
        FROM customers c
        JOIN invoices i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
        GROUP BY c.Country
        HAVING num_customers > 4
    ) AS country_sales
    ORDER BY total_sales
    LIMIT 2 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
        SELECT 
            c.Country,
            SUM(i.Total) AS total_sales
        FROM customers c
        JOIN invoices i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
        GROUP BY c.Country
        HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) > 4
    ) AS sub) % 2
    OFFSET (SELECT (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2 
            FROM (
                SELECT 
                    c.Country,
                    SUM(i.Total) AS total_sales
                FROM customers c
                JOIN invoices i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
                GROUP BY c.Country
                HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) > 4
            ) AS sub)
) AS median_values;

aggregation.selected_sql:
SELECT AVG(total_sales) AS median_total_sales
FROM (
    SELECT total_sales
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            'Unknown' AS Country,
            SUM(0) AS total_sales,
            COUNT(*) AS num_customers
        FROM albums
        GROUP BY 'Unknown'
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 4
    ) country_sales
    ORDER BY total_sales
    LIMIT 2 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
        SELECT 
            'Unknown' AS Country,
            SUM(0) AS total_sales
        FROM albums
        GROUP BY 'Unknown'
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 4
    ) AS cnt) % 2
    OFFSET (SELECT (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2
            FROM (
                SELECT 
                    'Unknown' AS Country,
                    SUM(0) AS total_sales
                FROM albums
                GROUP BY 'Unknown'
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 4
            ) AS sub)
) AS median_vals;

candidates.sql:
SELECT AVG(total_sales) AS median_total_sales
FROM (
    SELECT total_sales
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            'Unknown' AS Country,
            SUM(0) AS total_sales,
            COUNT(*) AS num_customers
        FROM albums
        GROUP BY 'Unknown'
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 4
    ) country_sales
    ORDER BY total_sales
    LIMIT 2 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
        SELECT 
            'Unknown' AS Country,
            SUM(0) AS total_sales
        FROM albums
        GROUP BY 'Unknown'
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 4
    ) AS cnt) % 2
    OFFSET (SELECT (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2
            FROM (
                SELECT 
                    'Unknown' AS Country,
                    SUM(0) AS total_sales
                FROM albums
                GROUP BY 'Unknown'
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 4
            ) AS sub)
) AS median_vals;

SELECT AVG(total_sales) AS median_total_sales
FROM (
    SELECT total_sales
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            a.Title AS Country,
            SUM(1) AS total_sales,
            COUNT(DISTINCT a.ArtistId) AS num_customers
        FROM albums a
        GROUP BY a.Title
        HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.ArtistId) > 4
    ) country_sales
    ORDER BY total_sales
    LIMIT 2 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
        SELECT 
            a.Title AS Country,
            SUM(1) AS total_sales
        FROM albums a
        GROUP BY a.Title
        HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.ArtistId) > 4
    ) AS cnt) % 2
    OFFSET (SELECT (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2
            FROM (
                SELECT 
                    a.Title AS Country,
                    SUM(1) AS total_sales
                FROM albums a
                GROUP BY a.Title
                HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.ArtistId) > 4
            ) AS sub)
) AS median_vals;
